title: Macrochiton lessertianum
taxonomy: Macrochiton lessertianum is classified within the family Cephalanthaceae. It belongs to the order Celastrales. The species is further categorized under the class Magnoliopsida. Macrochiton lessertianum falls within the division Magnoliophyta. Its classification is also supported by its placement in the kingdom Plantae.

The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. parts: Macrochiton lessertianum, also known as the "lessertia tree," is a plant species found in the tropical regions of Africa. The medicinal parts of Macrochiton lessertianum are primarily its roots and leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The roots are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, while the leaves are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. intro: Macrochiton lessertianum, also known as the African Spurge or Yellow Bitter Apple, is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. It has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Macrochiton lessertianum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the production of herbal teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
